subject: ap-government
title: Quantitative Analysis — Congressional Hearing Days Investigating Executive Branch, 1969–2014
stimulus: Source: Data from Kriner and Schickler, 2016. Bar graph showing Congressional Hearing Days in the House that investigated the Executive Branch from 1969 to 2014, coded by whether the House was controlled by the president's party or the opposing party.

parts.prompt: Using the bar graph, identify the president who faced the fewest number of investigative hearing days in a single year when the House was controlled by the opposing party.

scoring_points.description: Using the bar graph, identify the president who faced the fewest number of investigative hearing days in a single year when the House was controlled by the opposing party.

required_elements: Clinton faced the fewest number of investigative hearing days.
alternatives.correct_example: Clinton faced the fewest number of investigative hearing days.

parts.prompt: Describe the relationship between investigative hearing days and party control of the House and the presidency as shown in the bar graph.
parts.scoring_notes: Accept any accurate description of the relationship between party control and investigative hearing days based on the data.

scoring_points.description: Describe the relationship between investigative hearing days and party control of the House and the presidency as shown in the bar graph.

required_elements: When the opposing party controls the House, there are usually more hearings investigating the executive branch.
alternatives.correct_example: When the opposing party controls the House, there are usually more hearings investigating the executive branch.

required_elements: When the same party controls the House, there are usually fewer hearings investigating the executive branch.
alternatives.correct_example: When the same party controls the House, there are usually fewer hearings investigating the executive branch.

parts.prompt: Draw a conclusion about how the difference in congressional hearing days between 1970–1980 and 1990–2000 may have affected presidential power during those periods.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must draw a conclusion about presidential power based on the hearing day data — not just describe the data.

scoring_points.description: Draw a conclusion about how the difference in congressional hearing days between 1970–1980 and 1990–2000 may have affected presidential power during those periods.

required_elements: 1 pt: Draws a conclusion about the effect of hearing days on presidential power. Accept: between 1970 and 1980 there were more congressional hearing days than between 1990 and 2000, which might have led to a decline in presidential power during the 1970s; presidents faced less congressional scrutiny in the 1990s which may have allowed them to exercise more power.
alternatives.correct_example: 1 pt: Draws a conclusion about the effect of hearing days on presidential power. Accept: between 1970 and 1980 there were more congressional hearing days than between 1990 and 2000, which might have led to a decline in presidential power during the 1970s; presidents faced less congressional scrutiny in the 1990s which may have allowed them to exercise more power.

parts.prompt: Explain whether the data in the bar graph for the years in which the president's party controlled the House reflect Madison's ideas in The Federalist 51.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must engage with Madison's ideas in Federalist 51 about checks and balances and explain whether/how the data supports or contradicts those ideas. Accept either conclusion as long as it is supported with accurate reasoning.

scoring_points.description: Explain whether the data in the bar graph for the years in which the president's party controlled the House reflect Madison's ideas in The Federalist 51.

required_elements: Between 1970 and 1980, there were more congressional hearing days in the House than between 1990 and 2000, which might have led to a decline in presidential power. Explain whether the data in the bar graph for the years in which the president’s party controls the House reflect Madison’s ideas in The Federalist 51.
alternatives.correct_example: Between 1970 and 1980, there were more congressional hearing days in the House than between 1990 and 2000, which might have led to a decline in presidential power. Explain whether the data in the bar graph for the years in which the president’s party controls the House reflect Madison’s ideas in The Federalist 51.

required_elements: The Federalist 51 assumes that there will be checks and balances but does not take into consideration the role of political parties. Madison states that the legislative branch would serve as a check on the executive. But if one party controls both the House and the presidency, the interest of the House leadership in the welfare of the political party might be stronger than holding a president of their own party accountable. This would lead to fewer investigations of the executive branch, which would be contrary to the idea of checks in Federalist 51. Total for question 2
alternatives.correct_example: The Federalist 51 assumes that there will be checks and balances but does not take into consideration the role of political parties. Madison states that the legislative branch would serve as a check on the executive. But if one party controls both the House and the presidency, the interest of the House leadership in the welfare of the political party might be stronger than holding a president of their own party accountable. This would lead to fewer investigations of the executive branch, which would be contrary to the idea of checks in Federalist 51. Total for question 2
